articles.title: US-Iran Peace Agreement: Market Implications And Strategic Scenarios

articles.title: This Is The Most Powerful Bull Market Since 1999
articles.excerpt: Markets are in a powerful bull run, with all news interpreted positively and overbought conditions prevailing. I am increasing liquidity and taking profits, anticipating a pullback after earnings season and potential geopolitical resolution.

articles.title: Private-Sector Hiring Sped Up Last Month, ADP Says
articles.excerpt: Hiring by private-sector employers picked up in April, human-resources company ADP said, the latest hint that the labor market may have found more stability.

articles.title: S&P 500 Extends Record Run as Semis Soar, Oil Falls and Strait Traffic Flows
articles.excerpt: The S&P 500 (^GSPC) is set to extend a record run as crude tumbles and traders welcome signs the Iran conflict could be winding down.

articles.title: Private sector added 109,000 jobs in April, above expectations, ADP says
articles.excerpt: The figure reported on Wednesday is above economists' estimates of an increase of 99,000 jobs. The prior month's reading was revised lower to a gain of 62,000 jobs.

articles.title: Peace hopes, Disney earnings, dismantling Spirit Airlines and more in Morning Squawk
articles.excerpt: Here are five key things investors need to know to start the trading day.

articles.title: ADP says businesses create the most new jobs in 15 months. Labor market might be thawing.
articles.excerpt: ADP said U.S. businesses create 109,000 new jobs in April — the biggest increase in 15 months — in a sign a partly frozen labor market might be thawing out after months of unusually tepid hiring.

articles.title: Equity Factor Insights - April 2026
articles.excerpt: During Q1, the Middle East conflict materially impacted the macro environment and equity return trends. Unlike in Q4, larger equities were not consistently favored, which may reflect the change of leadership seen during the quarter with the macro shock and Tech moderation.

articles.title: Private payrolls rose by 109,000 in April, topping expectations, ADP says
articles.excerpt: Private companies added 109,000 jobs for the month, a step up from the 61,000 created in March and better than the Dow Jones consensus estimate for 84,000, ADP reported Wednesday. Education and health services again dominated, adding 61,000 jobs.

articles.title: China A50 Bullish Breakout Above 6-Month Resistance

articles.title: Saks May Exit Bankruptcy. Success Is The Next Question.
articles.excerpt: Saks Global Enterprises LLC may soon emerge from bankruptcy with less debt, fresh capital and a much smaller store base.

articles.title: The Attempt To Open Hormuz By Force Failed - Now What?
articles.excerpt: The Project Freedom was likely an initial attempt to open the Strait of Hormuz by force, and it failed. The Strait of Hormuz remains closed, with no end in sight, which increases the probability of an inflationary recession with each passing day.

articles.title: Nasdaq and S&P set for new record highs as Iran deal 'close'

articles.title: SPX Momentum Is Real: These Stocks Could Benefit
articles.excerpt: Stocks have been rallying lately, with the S&P 500 Index (SPX) gaining over 10% over the course of a month for the first time in about a year (not using calendar months, but rather any 21-trading day period).

articles.title: Global Payments quarterly profit rises on resilient consumer spending
articles.excerpt: Payment technology company Global Payments reported a rise in first-quarter adjusted profit on ​Wednesday, helped by resilient consumer spending despite ‌geopolitical tensions in the Middle East and a volatile economic backdrop.

articles.title: Brent crude falls below $100 a barrel amid Middle East peace hopes – business live

articles.title: Energy exec: Will take 6 months for Strait of Hormuz to return to normal
articles.excerpt: Torgrim Reitan, CFO of Norwegian energy giant Equinor, joins Europe Early Edition to discuss Q1 earnings, with profit rising more-than-expected amid tailwinds from higher output and oil prices.

articles.title: Surging Chip Stocks and Iran Peace Talks Put Wall Street on Pace for Further Records
articles.excerpt: U.S. stocks are outpacing their global rivals, with tech leading the charge. But worries about the war in Iran still linger.
